tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

content.text: {"number":58380,"title":"[AI Agent Server] Docs: README missing chat isolation, session ID header, metadata constraints, and updated validation behavior","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit 70b48842 (PR #58371) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.3 was released (triggered by the version-increment commit). The 1.0.0-beta.2 and 1.0.0-beta.3 releases added several user-facing behaviors — chat isolation enforcement, session ID header, metadata validation, and `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation — that are not documented in the README or troubleshooting section. ## Gaps Found - **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. This is a multi-tenancy security feature (added beta.2): when a response is created with this header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must include the same key; mismatched or missing keys return 404. Users implementing multi-tenant servers need to be aware of this behavior. - **`x-agent-session-id` response header** (added beta.2) is not documented. The library echoes the resolved session ID as a response header on all protocol endpoints; clients may need to forward or log this header. - **Metadata constraints** (added beta.2) are not documented: metadata maps are limited to 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 characters, value length ≤ 512 characters. Users passing metadata will receive 400 errors without knowing the limits unless they read the CHANGELOG. - **`previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` validation** (added beta.3): these values are now validated against the provider before the handler is invoked, returning 404/400 immediately. The Troubleshooting section does not list these new error cases. - **Troubleshooting section** is missing error entries for: isolation key mismatch → 404, invalid `previous_response_id` → 404, invalid `conversation.id` → 400, metadata constraint violation → 400. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit** **1a. Add a "Chat isolation" subsection under `## Key concepts`** (after the "Thread safety" section, before `## Examples`): ```markdown ### Chat isolation To enforce tenant isolation, clients may include an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header when creating a response. Once set, every subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems call for that response **must** include the same key. Calls with a mismatched or missing key return **404** (indistinguishable from not-found) to prevent cross-tenant information leakage. The isolation key value is never logged. ### Session ID header All protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, InputItems) echo the resolved session ID as the `x-agent-session-id` response header. The value corresponds to the `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable (or the deterministically derived session ID when a conversational context is available). Clients that need to correlate requests to a session can read this header from any response. ### Metadata constraints Metadata maps supplied in the request body are validated before the handler is invoked: - Maximum **16 keys** - Key length ≤ **64 characters** - Value length ≤ **512 characters** Requests that exceed these limits are rejected with **400 Bad Request** before your handler is called. ``` **1b. Expand the `### Common errors` subsection under `## Troubleshooting`** to add new entries: Add the following bullets to the existing list: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (conversation validation): `previous_response_id` was supplied but does not exist in the provider. Validation happens before your handler is invoked; the resolved history is cached for the request lifetime. - **400 Bad Request** (conversation validation): `conversation.id` was supplied but does not match the stored conversation record. - **400 Bad Request** (metadata): A metadata map exceeds 16 keys, or a key exceeds 64 characters, or a value exceeds 512 characters. - **404 Not Found** (isolation key): A GET, Cancel, DELETE, or InputItems request was made with a missing or mismatched `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` relative to the value used at creation time. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for this change. The documentation additions are conceptual/reference prose, not runnable sample code. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already has all required sections (Getting started → Key concepts → Examples → Troubleshooting → Next steps → Contributing). The edits above add subsections inside existing sections; no structural reorganization is needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm git diff shows no unintended changes to README snippet blocks 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected; verify the API listing is unchanged 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24661992977/agentic_workflow) · ● 388.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58380","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T10:51:08Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T10:51:08Z"}

{"total_count":130,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4090517017,"number":57183,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute We are deciding where to migrate, and we want to understand what will happen to Azure AI Assistants in general. And what are the differences between Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants and Azure.AI.OpenAI.

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.1.0-beta.1

### Describe the bug

Since the latest release of Azure.AI.Projects for Azure Foundry integration VisualStudioCredential within Visual Studio 2022 no longer works.

- VisualStudioCredential authentication failed: Process "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\Common7\IDE\CommonExtensions\Microsoft\Asal\TokenService\Microsoft.Asal.TokenService.exe" has failed with unexpected error: TS003: Error, TS004: Unable to get access token.  'Consent between first party application '04f0c124-f2bc-4f59-8241-bf6df9866bbd' and first party resource '18a66f5f-dbdf-4c17-9dd7-1634712a9cbe' must be configured via preauthorization - applications owned and operated by Microsoft must get approval from the API owner before requesting tokens for that API. Trace ID: 63dae0af-5e56-4ca5-b343-8700c30da500 Correlation ID: 003078b6-5f8a-4fe4-ac79-060af246ea7e Timestamp: 2025-05-25 20:12:25Z
For potential mitigation and additional information go to the following url: 'https://aka.ms/vspreautherror''.

This use to work prior to the new SDK as authentication used https://management.azure.com//.default for scopes. The new SDK uses https://ai.azure.com/.default and this fails with the above message.
I can use Azure CLI Credentials and the application will work as it uses a different ClientID but our development and test environments current rely on DefaultAzureCredentials and Visual Studio with Azure Service Authentication.
This all worked fine prior to the upgrade to the Azure.AI SDKs and the new structure. The root appears to be the VS App GUID not be registered with the Azure ML Services.

1.1.0-beta.1 (2025-05-21) - Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent
1.0.0-beta.9 (2025-05-16) - Azure.AI.Projects

### Expected behavior

As before - VisualStudioCredentials is able to authenticate against https://ai.azure.com/.default using VS App ID when using the latest version of Azure.AI.Projects and Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.

### Actual behavior

Any use of VisualStudioCredentials with the latest Azure.AI.Projects or Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent fails which makes the use of DefaultAzureCredentials difficult in test and development.

### Reproduction Steps

Use Visual Studio (VisualStudioCredentials) and the latest SDKs to access Azure AI Foundry

### Environment

Visual Studio 2022 - Enterprise or Community

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.5

### Describe the bug

From time to time we observe an issue with FunctionCalls where the Arguments seems to contain illegal JSON that can not be deserialized, which in turn throws an Exception in the `ProcessToolCalls` function of `OpenTelemetryScope`.

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Telemetry/OpenTelemetryScope.cs#L1147

### Expected behavior

* The issue (I would say) is that there is an assumption on arguments to be valid JSON. 
* The error should not occur in the `OpenTelemetryScope`, before the user has any chance to handle it.
* Also, shouldn't agents enfore valid JSON responses? (structured outputs or something, am I doing something wrong?)

### Actual behavior

An exception when function call arguments are deserialized.

```

{"total_count":23,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4090517017,"number":57183,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

Sometimes (not always) when I iterate over `AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate>` I get an error stating:

```
The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true. LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
```

* I have the same problem with both `gpt-4.1` and `gpt-5`
* **Worth pointing out that it works if I use the same agent and prompt from the AI Foundry Agent  playground**

The error comes after `UpdateKind` = RunInProgress

```csharp
PersistentThreadMessage messageResponse = _client.Messages.CreateMessage(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    role: MessageRole.User,
    content: request.Prompt);

AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate> stream = _client.Runs.CreateRunStreamingAsync(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    agentId: _agent.Id
);

await foreach (StreamingUpdate streamingUpdate in stream) // This is where the error occurs after RunInProgress
{
      _logger.LogInformation(streamingUpdate.UpdateKind.ToString());
}
```

### Expected behavior

If this is due to a problem with the payload, such as an empty response from the LLM or a throttling error, I would expect to see this error

### Actual behavior

I assume the response from the LLM is not in an expected format and that this is an internal bug.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-filed documentation gap issue for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.3, noting that the README is missing documentation for chat isolation enforcement, session ID response header, metadata validation constraints, and updated `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation behavior introduced in beta.2 and beta.3.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.3)
**Affected API:** README / Troubleshooting section
**Scenarios:**
- Users implementing multi-tenant servers are unaware of the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header and its 404-on-mismatch behavior
- Clients not forwarding or logging the `x-agent-session-id` response header may miss session correlation information
- Users supplying metadata may receive unexpected 400 errors without knowledge of the 16-key/64-char/512-char limits
- Troubleshooting section does not document new 404/400 cases added in beta.2 and beta.3

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` to document the above behaviors and expand the Troubleshooting section with new error entries.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no code defect is reported. The issue body includes a detailed implementation guide with exact markdown to add.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review CHANGELOG entries for beta.2 and beta.3 of `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` to confirm all behavioral changes are captured
2. Apply the README edits described in the issue body (chat isolation, session ID header, metadata constraints, troubleshooting entries)
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` and `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` to verify no unintended changes
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a client-side SDK library (not Azure.ResourceManager.* or Azure.Provisioning.*), and the issue concerns SDK documentation behavior
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — CODEOWNERS maps `/sdk/agentserver/` to `%AI Agents` (line 85); confirmed by quality reference issues #57183, #55585, #55561 all using "Client" + "AI Agents"
- **Confidence:** High — package, service directory, and CODEOWNERS entry are unambiguous; multiple quality reference issues corroborate the label pair
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — service label "AI Agents" matched this entry; scanning bottom-to-top, no more-specific multi-label entry for "AI Agents" exists
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko, @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners-only path); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not "customer-reported")
- **Scan notes:** All management-specific entries (lines 912+) require "Mgmt" — not applicable. The `%AI Model Inference` (line 94) and `%AI Projects` (line 100) entries don't match "AI Agents". First match encountered bottom-to-top was line 85.
</details>
